name: nomenklatura

metadata.name: nomenklatura
metadata.summary: Make record linkages in followthemoney data.

documentation:
# nomenklatura

Nomenklatura de-duplicates and integrates different [Follow the Money](https://followthemoney.rtfd.org/) entities. It serves to clean up messy data and to find links between different datasets.

![screenshot](./docs/screenshot.png)

## Usage

You can install `nomenklatura` via PyPI:

```bash
$ pip install nomenklatura
```

### Command-line usage

Much of the functionality of `nomenklatura` can be used as a command-line tool. In the following example, we'll assume that you have a file containing [Follow the Money](https://followthemoney.rtfd.org/) entities in your local directory, named `entities.ijson`. If you just want try it out, you can use the file `tests/fixtures/donations.ijson` in this repository for testing (it contains German campaign finance data).

With the file in place, you will cross-reference the entities to generate de-duplication candidates, then run the interactive de-duplication UI in your console, and eventually apply the judgements to generate a new file with merged entities:

```bash
# generate merge candidates using an in-memory index:
$ nomenklatura xref -r resolver.json entities.ijson
# note there is now a new file, `resolver.json` that contains de-duplication info.
$ nomenklatura dedupe -r resolver.json entites.ijson
# will pop up a user interface.
$ nomenklatura apply entities.ijson -o merged.ijson -r resolver.json
# de-duplicated data goes into `merged.ijson`:
$ cat entities.ijson | wc -l 
474
$ cat merged.ijson | wc -l 
468 
```

### Programmatic usage

The command-line use of `nomenklatura` is targeted at small datasets which need to be de-duplicated. For more involved scenarios, the package also offers a Python API which can be used to control the semantics of de-duplication.

* `nomenklatura.Dataset` - implements a basic dataset for describing a set of entities.
* `nomenklatura.Store` - a general purpose access mechanism for entities. By default, a store is used to access entity data stored in files as an in-memory cache, but the store can be subclassed to work with entities from a database system.
* `nomenklatura.Index` - a full-text in-memory search index for FtM entities. In the application, this is used to block de-duplication candidates, but the index can also be used to drive an API etc.
* `nomenklatura.index.TantivyIndex` - a wrapper around Tantivy for indexing and matching FtM entities.
* `nomenklatura.Resolver` - the core of the de-duplication process, the resolver is essentially a graph with edges made out of entity judgements. The resolver can be used to store judgements or get the canonical ID for a given entity.

All of the API classes have extensive type annotations, which should make their integration in any modern Python API simpler.

## Design

This package offers an implementation of an in-memory data deduplication framework centered around the FtM data model. The idea is the following workflow:

* Accept FtM-shaped entities from a given loader (e.g. a JSON file, or a database)
* Build an in-memory inverted index of the entities for dedupe blocking
* Generate merge candidates using the blocking index and FtM compare
* Provide a file-based storage format for merge challenges and decisions
* Provide a text-based user interface to let users make merge decisions

Later on, the following might be added:

* A web application to let users make merge decisions on the web

### Resolver graph

The key implementation detail of nomenklatura is the `Resolver`, a graph structure that
manages user decisions regarding entity identity. Edges are `Judgements` of whether
two entity IDs are the same, not the same, or undecided. The resolver implements an
algorithm for computing connected components, which can the be used to find the best
available ID for a cluster of entities. It can also be used to evaluate transitive
judgements, e.g. if A <> B, and B = C, then we don't need to ask if A = C.

## Reading

* https://dedupe.readthedocs.org/en/latest/
* https://github.com/OpenRefine/OpenRefine/wiki/Reconcilable-Data-Sources
* https://github.com/OpenRefine/OpenRefine/wiki/Clustering-In-Depth
* https://github.com/OpenRefine/OpenRefine/wiki/Reconciliation-Service-API


## Contact, contributions etc.

This codebase is licensed under the terms of an MIT license (see LICENSE).

We're keen for any contributions, bug fixes and feature suggestions, please use the GitHub issue tracker for this repository. 

Nomenklatura is currently developed thanks to a Prototypefund grant for [OpenSanctions](https://opensanctions.org). Previous iterations of the package were developed with support from [Knight-Mozilla OpenNews](http://opennews.org) and the [Open Knowledge Foundation Labs](http://okfnlabs.org).

modules.name: tests.store.test_stores
documentation: Test if the different store implementations all behave the same.

functions.name: tests.index.test_index.test_match_score
documentation: Match an entity that isn't itself in the index

functions.name: tests.index.test_index.test_top_match_matches_strong_pairs
documentation: Pairs with high scores are each others' top matches

documentation: Match an entity that isn't itself in the index

functions.name: tests.index.test_tantivy_index.test_name_variations
documentation:
More variations of the same name doesn't increase the score

This is to make sure that entities where many similar but not identical
names have been merged in don't end up bumping up scores of less relevant
entities with similar names.

functions.name: tests.enrich.test_wikidata.wd_read_response
documentation:
Read a local file if it exists, otherwise download it. This is not
so much a mocker as a test disk cache.

documentation: Delete all data associated with a prefix of the store.

documentation: Copy all data from another store into this one.

A store implementation which is built to store fully resolved entities. This
implementation is not designed to be updated, and cannot store individual statements.

functions.name: nomenklatura.store.load_entity_file_store
documentation: Create a simple in-memory store by reading FtM entities from a file path.

documentation: List all versions of a dataset present in the store.

methods.name: nomenklatura.store.versioned.VersionedRedisStore.has_version
documentation: Check if a specific version of a dataset exists in the store.

methods.name: nomenklatura.store.versioned.VersionedRedisStore.release_version
documentation:
Release the given version of the dataset (i.e. tag it as the latest
version in the relevant lookup key).

methods.name: nomenklatura.store.versioned.VersionedRedisStore.drop_version
documentation: Delete all data associated with a specific version of a dataset.

methods.name: nomenklatura.store.versioned.VersionedRedisWriter.release
documentation:
Release the current version of the dataset (i.e. tag it as the latest
version in the relevant lookup key).

methods.name: nomenklatura.store.versioned.VersionedRedisView.get_timestamps
documentation:
Get the first seen timestamps associated with all statements of an entity.

Returns a dictionary mapping statement IDs to their first seen timestamps.
This can be used by an ETL to generate continuous entity histories.

methods.name: nomenklatura.store.versioned.VersionedRedisView.statements
documentation:
Iterate over all statements in the view. If `resolve` is set to `True`,
canonical IDs are applied to the statement and its value.

NOTE: The `external` flag of the view will be used to filter statements, too.

classes.name: nomenklatura.store.base.Store
documentation:
A data storage and retrieval mechanism for statement-based entity data.
Essentially, this is a triple store which can be implemented using various
backends.

classes.name: nomenklatura.store.base.Writer
documentation: Bulk writing operations.

classes.name: nomenklatura.statement.statement.Statement

methods.name: nomenklatura.statement.statement.Statement.clone
documentation: Make a deep copy of the given statement.

methods.name: nomenklatura.statement.statement.Statement.make_key
documentation: Hash the key properties of a statement record to make a unique ID.

A single statement about a property relevant to an entity.

For example, this could be useddocker to say: "In dataset A, entity X has the
property `name` set to 'John Smith'. I first observed this at K, and last
saw it at L."

Null property values are not supported. This might need to change if we
want to support making property-less entities.

functions.name: nomenklatura.store.level.b
documentation: Encode a string to bytes.

A unit of entities. A dataset is a set of data, sez W3C.

methods.name: nomenklatura.resolver.linker.Linker.get_referents
documentation:
Get all the non-canonical entity identifiers which refer to a given
canonical identifier.

methods.name: nomenklatura.resolver.linker.Linker.apply
documentation:
Replace all entity references in a given proxy with their canonical
identifiers. This is essentially the harmonisation post de-dupe.

A class to manage the canonicalisation of entities. This stores only the positive
merges of entities and is used as a lightweight way to apply the harmonisation
post de-duplication.

Return the number of elements in the intersection of two lists, accounting

This is less memory-consuming than the full resolver object.

methods.name: nomenklatura.resolver.resolver.Resolver.canonicals

Check if the two IDs could be merged, i.e. if there's no existing
judgement.

methods.name: nomenklatura.resolver.resolver.Resolver._get_suggested
documentation: Get all NO_JUDGEMENT edges in descending order of score.

methods.name: nomenklatura.resolver.resolver.Resolver.suggest
documentation:
Make a NO_JUDGEMENT link between two identifiers to suggest that a user
should make a decision about whether they are the same or not.

methods.name: nomenklatura.resolver.resolver.Resolver._remove_edge
documentation: Remove an edge from the graph.

methods.name: nomenklatura.resolver.resolver.Resolver._remove_node
documentation: Remove a node from the graph.

methods.name: nomenklatura.resolver.resolver.Resolver.remove
documentation: Remove all edges linking to the given node from the graph.

methods.name: nomenklatura.resolver.resolver.Resolver.explode
documentation:
Dissolve all edges linked to the cluster to which the node belongs.
This is the hard way to make sure we re-do context once we realise
there's been a mistake.

methods.name: nomenklatura.resolver.resolver.Resolver.prune
documentation:
Remove suggested (i.e. NO_JUDGEMENT) edges, keep only the n with the
highest score. This also checks if a transitive judgement has been
established in the mean time and removes those candidates.

methods.name: nomenklatura.resolver.resolver.Resolver.save
documentation: Store the resolver adjacency list to a plain text JSON list.

methods.name: nomenklatura.resolver.resolver.Resolver.load_linker
documentation:
Load a resolver file and return a linker object, without ever instantiating
a full resolver object.

documentation:
A generator of each

- index field name and
-  the set of normalised but not tokenised values for that field

for the given entity.

documentation: A generator of queries for the given index field and set of values.

methods.name: nomenklatura.index.tantivy_index.TantivyIndex.pairs

Compare all matchable entities in the index and return pairs in order of
similarity.

methods.name: nomenklatura.index.entry.Entry.add
documentation: Mark the given entity as relevant to the entry's token.

methods.name: nomenklatura.index.entry.Entry.frequencies
documentation:
Term Frequency (TF) for each entity in this entry.

TF being the number of occurrences of this token in the entity divided
by the total number of tokens in the entity (scoped to this field).
A set of entities and a weight associated with a given term in the index.

classes.name: nomenklatura.index.entry.Field
documentation: Index of all tokens of the same type.

classes.name: nomenklatura.index.index.Index

methods.name: nomenklatura.index.index.Index.index
documentation:
Index one entity. This is not idempotent, you need to remove the
entity before re-indexing it.

methods.name: nomenklatura.index.index.Index.build
documentation: Index all entities in the dataset.

methods.name: nomenklatura.index.index.Index.pairs
documentation:
A second method of doing xref: summing up the pairwise match value
for all entities linearly. This uses a lot of memory but is really
fast.

The score of each pair is the the sum of the product of term frequencies for
each co-occurring token in each field of the pair.

We skip any tokens with more than 100 entities.

methods.name: nomenklatura.index.index.Index.match
documentation:
Match an entity against the index, returning a list of
(entity_id, score) pairs.

methods.name: nomenklatura.index.index.Index.to_dict
documentation: Prepare an index for pickling.

methods.name: nomenklatura.index.index.Index.from_dict
documentation: Restore a pickled index.

An in-memory search index to match entities against a given dataset.

For each field in the dataset, the index stores the IDs which contains each
token, along with the absolute frequency of each token in the document.

functions.name: nomenklatura.publish.dates.simplify_dates
documentation:
If an entity has multiple values for a date field, you may
want to remove all those that are prefixes of others. For example,
if a Person has both a birthDate of 1990 and of 1990-05-01, we'd
want to drop the mention of 1990.

functions.name: nomenklatura.publish.dates.remove_prefix_date_values
documentation: See `remove_prefix_dates`.

functions.name: nomenklatura.publish.edges.simplify_undirected
documentation:
Simplify undirected edges by removing duplicate entity IDs on both
ends.

The user-facing label to be used for this entity. This checks a list
of properties defined by the schema (caption) and returns the first
available value. If no caption is available, return the schema label.

methods.name: nomenklatura.entity.CompositeEntity.unsafe_add

An entity object that can link to a set of datasets that it is sourced from.

classes.name: nomenklatura.stream.StreamEntity
documentation:
This is used to retain extra attributes in the entity when doing streaming
command-line operations where the statement data is not available.

functions.name: nomenklatura.matching.name_based.names.soundex_name_parts

Compare two sets of name parts using the Jaro-Winkler string similarity
algorithm.

classes.name: nomenklatura.matching.name_based.model.NameMatcher
documentation:
An algorithm that matches on entity name, using phonetic comparisons and edit
distance to generate potential matches. This implementation is vaguely based on
the behaviour proposed by the US OFAC documentation (FAQ #249).

classes.name: nomenklatura.matching.name_based.model.NameQualifiedMatcher
documentation:
Same as the name-based algorithm, but scores will be reduced if a mis-match
of birth dates and nationalities is found for persons, or different
tax/registration identifiers are included for organizations and companies.

functions.name: nomenklatura.matching.regression_v2.names.first_name_match
documentation: Matching first/given name between the two entities.

functions.name: nomenklatura.matching.regression_v2.names.name_part_soundex
documentation: Check for overlap of phonetic forms of the names.

functions.name: nomenklatura.matching.regression_v2.names.name_numbers
documentation: Find if names contain numbers, score if the numbers are different.

documentation: Same place of birth.

Matching identifiers (e.g. registration or tax numbers) between two

functions.name: nomenklatura.matching.get_algorithm

classes.name: nomenklatura.matching.logic.LogicV1
documentation:
A rule-based matching system that generates a set of basic scores via
name and identifier-based matching, and then qualifies that score using
supporting or contradicting features of the two entities.

functions.name: nomenklatura.matching.compare.countries.country_mismatch

functions.name: nomenklatura.matching.compare.dates.dob_year_disjoint
documentation: The birth date of the two entities is not the same.

functions.name: nomenklatura.matching.compare.addresses._address_match
documentation: Text similarity between addresses.

functions.name: nomenklatura.matching.compare.addresses.address_entity_match
documentation: Two address entities relate to similar addresses.

functions.name: nomenklatura.matching.compare.addresses.address_prop_match
documentation: Two entities have similar stated addresses.

Two non-person entities have similar fingerprinted names. This includes

functions.name: nomenklatura.matching.compare.phonetic._clean_phonetic_entity
documentation: Normalize a legal entity name without transliteration.
def _clean_phonetic_person(original: str) -> Optional[str]:
"""Normalize a person name without transliteration."""
if not is_modern_alphabet(original):
return None
text = clean_entity_prefix(original)
return clean_name_ascii(text)

functions.name: nomenklatura.matching.compare.phonetic._token_names_compare
documentation: def _phonetic_person_tokens(token: str) -> List[str]:
words: List[str] = []
for word in name_words(_clean_phonetic_person(token), min_length=2):
words.append(phonetic_token(word))
return words

functions.name: nomenklatura.matching.compare.phonetic.person_name_phonetic_match
documentation: Two persons have similar names, using a phonetic algorithm.

functions.name: nomenklatura.matching.compare.phonetic.name_metaphone_match
documentation:
Two entities (person and non-person) have similar names, using the metaphone
algorithm.

functions.name: nomenklatura.matching.compare.phonetic.name_soundex_match
documentation:
Two entities (person and non-person) have similar names, using the soundex

methods.name: nomenklatura.enrich.aleph.AlephEnricher.match
documentation: def enrich_entity(self, entity):
url = self.api._make_url("match")
for page in range(10):
data = self.post_match(url, entity)
for res in data.get("results", []):
proxy = self.convert_entity(res)
yield self.make_match(entity, proxy)
url = data.get("next")
if url is None:
break
def expand_entity(self, entity):
for url in entity.get("alephUrl", quiet=True):
data = self.get_api(url)
yield from self.convert_nested(data)
_, entity_id = url.rsplit("/", 1)
filters = (("entities", entity_id),)
search_api = self.api._make_url("entities", filters=filters)
while True:
res = self.get_api(search_api)
for data in ensure_list(res.get("results")):
yield from self.convert_nested(data)
search_api = res.get("next")
if search_api is None:
break

functions.name: nomenklatura.enrich.match
documentation: nk match -i entities.json -o entities-with-matches.json -r resolver.json

nk dedupe -i entities-with-matches.json -r resolver.json

methods.name: nomenklatura.enrich.opencorporates.OpenCorporatesEnricher.make_entity_id
documentation: def expand_entity(self, entity):
for url in entity.get("opencorporatesUrl", quiet=True):
url = self.make_url(url)
data = self.get_api(url).get("results", {})
if "company" in data:
yield from self.expand_company(entity, data)
if "officer" in data:
yield from self.expand_officer(data, officer=entity)

methods.name: nomenklatura.enrich.opencorporates.OpenCorporatesEnricher.search_companies
documentation: def officer_entity(self, data, entity=None):
if "officer" in data:
data = ensure_dict(data.get("officer", data))
person = data.get("occupation") or data.get("date_of_birth")
schema = "Person" if person else "LegalEntity"
entity = model.make_entity(schema)
entity.make_id(data.get("opencorporates_url"))
entity.add("name", data.get("name"))
entity.add("country", data.get("nationality"))
entity.add("jurisdiction", data.get("jurisdiction_code"))
entity.add("address", data.get("address"))
entity.add("birthDate", data.get("date_of_birth"), quiet=True)
entity.add("position", data.get("occupation"), quiet=True)
entity.add("opencorporatesUrl", data.get("opencorporates_url"))
source = data.get("source", {})
entity.add("publisher", source.get("publisher"))
entity.add("publisherUrl", source.get("url"))
entity.add("retrievedAt", source.get("retrieved_at"))
return entity
